how_to_use_with_gemini: Copy the entire contents of this file into Gemini (one message). Then ask questions such as: how do I run segmentation? what does prepare_coco_val5k do? why are train.txt paths absolute? how do I fix CUDA OOM? what classes does the segmenter use? Gemini should answer only from this context plus general ML knowledge, and say when something is not specified here.

project.name: Bayza
project.description: A small Python project with Ultralytics YOLOv8-seg: object detection (bounding boxes, class names, confidence) plus instance segmentation (semi-transparent masks and contours) for people and cars; also download a manageable COCO 2017 validation subset for training and run short fine-tuning experiments.
project.repository_root_convention: All scripts under scripts/ resolve paths from the repository root using ROOT = Path(__file__).resolve().parents[1]. Users should run commands from the repo root so defaults stay correct.

libraries.name: ultralytics
libraries.role: YOLOv8-seg model load, predict, train APIs

libraries.name: opencv-python
libraries.role: Read/write images, resize masks, contours for outlines

stack.optional_hardware: CUDA GPU recommended for training scripts; CPU possible but slow. Windows-friendly defaults use workers=0 in training scripts.

directory_layout.scripts: Runnable entry points (all Python code for this project).
directory_layout.weights: Local .pt checkpoints (yolov8s-seg.pt, yolov8n-seg.pt, yolo11n.pt, etc.). Folder tracked via weights/.gitkeep; weight files are gitignored.
directory_layout.data/samples: Optional demo input images; segment_humans_cars can download bus.jpg and zidane.jpg from Ultralytics assets.
directory_layout.datasets: Prepared data: coco_val5k subset, coco128-seg, generated YAMLs (e.g. coco_val5k_bayza.yaml, coco128_bayza.yaml).
directory_layout.outputs: segment_humans_cars writes overlaid JPEGs named seg_<stem>.jpg.
directory_layout.outputs_finetuned: Optional separate output folder name from earlier experiments (gitignored).
directory_layout.runs: Ultralytics training outputs (weights, plots, args.yaml, etc.; gitignored).

segmentation_demo.goal: Run YOLOv8-seg for person and car only: draw bounding boxes with labels and scores (object detection), then semi-transparent instance masks and thin contours (instance segmentation).
segmentation_demo.pipeline_summary: Load image with OpenCV; YOLO.predict with classes=[0,2]; if masks exist, resize each mask to image size, alpha-blend fill, findContours for edges; then draw axis-aligned boxes and text from results.boxes on top (unless --no-boxes); save JPEG.

cli_arguments.--weights: Path to .pt; default ROOT/weights/yolov8s-seg.pt. Ultralytics downloads missing weights on first use.
cli_arguments.--source: Zero or more image paths or folders; globs jpg/jpeg/png/webp/bmp in folders.
cli_arguments.--download-samples: Flag: download default sample images into --data-dir.
cli_arguments.--no-boxes: If set, skip drawing bounding boxes and labels (masks and contours only).
cli_arguments.--data-dir: Sample folder when no --source; default ROOT/data/samples.

segmentation_demo.default_behavior_without_source: If --source omitted: ensure data-dir exists; if empty, download default samples; then segment all images in data-dir.
segmentation_demo.http_headers: Sample downloads use urllib with a simple User-Agent string identifying educational use.

coco_val5k_preparation.goal: Avoid full COCO train (~20 GB) by using COCO 2017 val images (~5k, ~815 MB zip) plus segment labels from Ultralytics zip; layout under datasets/coco_val5k/.
coco_val5k_preparation.split: Random seed 42; 90% train / 10% val by image stem; both splits reference the same images folder but disjoint file lists.
coco_val5k_preparation.why_absolute_paths_in_txt: train.txt and val.txt list absolute POSIX paths to .jpg files so Ultralytics resolves labels correctly on Windows regardless of current working directory.
coco_val5k_preparation.yaml_generation: Derived from Ultralytics coco.yaml template: path set to datasets/coco_val5k root, train/val point to train.txt and val.txt.
coco_val5k_preparation.exported_function: ensure_coco_val5k_dataset(skip_image_download?, skip_label_download?) returns Path to the data YAML; used by train_run_30min.py.
coco_val5k_preparation.large_artifacts_gitignored: Downloaded zips under datasets/coco_val5k/_*.zip, _stage_extract/, and images/ folder are gitignored in this repo policy.

training_time_capped.goal: Fine-tune YOLOv8-seg on the coco_val5k layout with a GPU time budget.
training_time_capped.data_flow: Unless --skip-data-prep, calls ensure_coco_val5k_dataset() then passes resulting YAML to YOLO.train.
training_time_capped.ultralytics_time_argument: model.train(..., time=args.train_hours, ...) caps wall-time training; default train_hours=0.5 (30 minutes). Epochs default high (999) so time limit usually stops first.
training_time_capped.--skip_data_prep: Skip downloads; requires existing YAML_OUT from prepare_coco_val5k.

training_coco128_lite.goal: Quick fine-tune on tiny coco128-seg (~128 images) for fast iteration.
training_coco128_lite.data_flow: If --data is local/bayza/project (case-insensitive), downloads coco128-seg.zip to datasets/, extracts under datasets/, writes datasets/coco128_bayza.yaml with absolute path to local coco root (from ultralytics template minus download lines). Otherwise --data can be any path or Ultralytics dataset yaml name.
training_coco128_lite.scope_note: Fine-tuning updates all 80 COCO classes present in labels, not only person and car.

report_figures.purpose: Read Ultralytics results.csv and write publication-style PNGs (300 dpi) into reports/: train vs val losses (2x2), bounding-box metrics, mask metrics, learning rate, multi-panel dashboard, and optional side-by-side qualitative input vs segmentation output.
report_figures.defaults: Processes runs/segment/train30_coco_val5k/results.csv and runs/segment/finetune_lite_v2/results.csv if present; qualitative pair defaults to data/samples/bus.jpg vs outputs/seg_bus.jpg.

gitignore_policy_summary: Ignores Python caches, venvs, all .pt weights, runs/, outputs/, outputs_finetuned/, the entire datasets/ tree (COCO subsets, labels, zips, generated YAMLs — recreate locally), common IDE/OS junk.
readme_location: README.md contains setup venv/pip, student line, script table, and layout bullets.
common_user_questions_covered: Where do I put weights? → weights/
Why no images in git? → Large data gitignored; run prepare script or bring your own images.
How to segment one image? → python scripts/segment_humans_cars.py --source path/to/img.jpg
OOM during training? → Lower --batch or --imgsz; use smaller model (n vs s).
Only person and car in demo? → Yes in segment_humans_cars; training uses full COCO class sets in dataset YAMLs.
licensing_note: Ultralytics YOLO is used (check Ultralytics license for commercial use). COCO dataset has its own terms. This JSON does not replace those licenses.
